title: Inter container communication is not working with docker-compose and podman as a backend
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am trying to host a container architecture using ```docker-compose``` with the podman backend. The docker-compose.yml looks somewhat like this: 

```
services:
  db:
    image: db
    container_name: db
    ports:
      - 7474:7474
      - 7687:7687
    networks:
      - com
  backend:
    image: backend
    container_name: backend
    ports:
      - 8080:8080
    environment:
      # Lookup host of db on docker-compose network
      - DB_HOST=db:7687
    depends_on:
      - db
    networks:
      - com
networks:
  com: {}
```
You can see that I am creating a database and a backend that is trying to access said database over the docker-compose network. This is working when run with the docker backend, but db does not resolve when using the podman backend. 

I also tried accessing the database over localhost by setting DB_HOST to localhost:7678 as I assumed that podman would create all the containers in one pod and the would share the localhost, but this did not work either.  Running ```podman pod ls``` shows me no pods at all. Maybe this is the reason for this. 

Bottom line is that the inter container communication is not working. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run a similar docker-compose architecture

2. Try to access the first container from the second container using the docker-compose service name or localhost.

**Describe the results you received:**

Service name cannot be resolved, using localhost does result in timeouts as the container cannot be found.

**Describe the results you expected:**

One container can actually reach the other container by using the service name or localhost.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version: 3.0.2-dev
API Version: 3.0.0
Go Version: go1.15.13
Built: Tue Jun 8 09:52:06 2021
OS/Arch: linux/amd64
```

```
podman-3.0.1-7.module+e18.4.0+11311+9da8acfb.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes I checked the troubleshooting guide, but didn't find the issue. I know that we are using an older version of podman, but we have no possibility to upgrade podman on this VM. 

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Do you have the dnsname plugin installed (`rpm -q podman-plugins`)?
docker-compose will never create pods, it should work exactly like docker.
Yeah, I saw this issue https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9292 and installed it: ```podman-plugins03-0.1-7.module+e18.4.0+11311+9da8acfb.x86_64```.
Can you try with a more recent Podman release? 3.0.x was the first release that supported Compose and we've merged a lot of fixes since then.
Upgrading to podman version 3.2.3 did indeed fix the problem

I have the same problem, but am using podman version 3.3.1:
version: '3.7'
services:
  zabbix_proxy:
    build:
      context: .
    ports:
      - 10055:10051
    env_file:
      - env-file.env
    container_name: crl_ZabbixProxy_5.4_10055
    volumes:
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/enc:/var/lib/zabbix/enc:z
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/externalscripts:/usr/lib/zabbix/externalscripts:z
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/mibs:/var/lib/zabbix/mibs:z
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/modules:/var/lib/zabbix/modules:z
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/snmptraps:/var/lib/zabbix/snmptraps:z
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/ssh_keys:/var/lib/zabbix/ssh_keys:z
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/ssl_ca:/var/lib/zabbix/ssl/ssl_ca:z
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/ssl_certs:/var/lib/zabbix/ssl/certs:z
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/ssl_keys:/var/lib/zabbix/ssl/keys:z
    networks:
      - crl_ZabbixProxy_5.4_10055_network
  mysql_db_crl:
    container_name: mysql_crl_ZabbixProxy_5.4_10055
    image: mariadb:latest
    ports:
      - 3306:3306
    volumes:
      - /opt/zabbix/zabbix-files/mysql-proxy-zabbix-crl-10055/mysql:/var/lib/mysql:z
    environment:
      - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=zabbix
      - MYSQL_DATABASE=zabbix
    networks:
      - crl_ZabbixProxy_5.4_10055_network

networks:
  crl_ZabbixProxy_5.4_10055_network:
    driver: bridge

On docker it works fine
